name: Kris Kristofferson
title: Last known photo of Kris Kristofferson, posted by Tanya Tucker to Instagram on April 11, 2024, from his longtime Maui ranch — about five and a half months before his death.
description: The last known photo of Kris Kristofferson was shared by country singer Tanya Tucker on Instagram on April 11, 2024. It was taken at his longtime Maui ranch in Hawaii — a 25-acre property he had purchased in 1984 for $120,000 and called home for the next four decades. Kristofferson had retired from performing in 2021 and lived quietly on the island, once describing the place as “an old samurai poet warrior’s house. I’m his groundskeeper.” He died at that same home on September 28, 2024, of natural causes. He was 88 years old.

lyrics.text:
I was a sailor
I was born upon the tide
And with the sea I did abide
I sailed a schooner around the Horn to Mexico
I went aloft and furled the mainsail in a blow
And when the yards broke off they said that I got killed
But I am living still

bio: Kris Kristofferson was an American singer-songwriter and actor, and a pioneering figure in outlaw country music. His songs — including “Me and Bobby McGee,” “Help Me Make It Through the Night,” “Sunday Mornin’ Comin’ Down,” and “For the Good Times” — became standards covered by Janis Joplin, Johnny Cash, Sammi Smith, and many others. With Cash, Willie Nelson, and Waylon Jennings he formed the supergroup The Highwaymen. As an actor he co-starred with Barbra Streisand in A Star Is Born (1976), played the title role in Sam Peckinpah’s Pat Garrett and Billy the Kid (1973), and appeared as Whistler in the Blade film trilogy.
